title: Swarved Mahamandir
tagline: Swarved Mahamandir — spiritual town of Uttar Pradesh

overview.short: Swarved Mahamandir is a charitable meditation centre built in Varanasi and inaugurated by Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi and with its seven-storey claims to be the biggest med
overview.description: Swarved Mahamandir is a charitable meditation centre built in Varanasi and inaugurated by Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi and with its seven-storey claims to be the biggest meditation centre in the World. The centre is built with an estimated cost of Rs 100 crores and has a seating capacity of 20,000 people at a time. Around 3137 verses of Swarveda are carved on the walls of the mahamandir which is covered with Makarana Marble.

topPlaces.name: Umarha
topPlaces.description: Umaraha is a census town in Varanasi tehsil of Varanasi district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. The census town & village falls under the Umaraha gram panchayat.

topPlaces.name: Vyaspur
topPlaces.description: Vyaspur is a village in Chiraigaon block of Varanasi district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It is about 219 kilometers from the state capital Lucknow and 809 kilometers from the national capital Delhi.

topPlaces.name: Sarnath Deer Park
topPlaces.description: Deer Park (Hindi: डियर पार्क) is a mini zoo in Sarnath, Varanasi, Uttar Pradesh, India. Deer Park is also known as Sarnath Deer Park (Hindi: सारनाथ डियर पार्क).

topPlaces.name: Dhamek Stupa
topPlaces.description: Dhamek Stupa (also spelled Dhamekh and Dhamekha) is a massive stupa located in Deer Park at Sarnath in the state of Uttar Pradesh, India. One of the eight most important pilgrimage sites for Buddhists, the Dhamek Stupa marks the location where the Buddha gave his first teaching to his first five disciples Kaundinya, Assaji, Bhaddiya, Vappa and Mahanama.

topPlaces.name: Sarnath Jain Tirth
topPlaces.description: Sarnath Jain Tirth, also called the Shreyanshnath Jain Pilgrimage, is a group of Jain temples in Sarnath. They are located near Dhamek Stupa.

topPlaces.name: Sarnath
topPlaces.description: Sarnath (also known as Deer Park, Sarangnath, Isipatana Deer Park, Rishipattana, Migadaya, or Mrigadava) is a town northeast of Varanasi, in Uttar Pradesh, India. It was here that the Buddha gave his first sermon after attaining enlightenment at Bodh Gaya.

topPlaces.name: Sarnath Museum
topPlaces.description: Sarnath Museum, or Archaeological Museum Sarnath, is the oldest site museum of the Archaeological Survey of India. It houses the findings and excavations at the archaeological site of Sarnath, by the Archaeological Survey of India.

topPlaces.name: Chaukhandi Stupa
topPlaces.description: Chaukhandi Stupa is a Buddhist stupa in Sarnath located 8 kilometres from Cantt Railway Station in Varanasi, Uttar Pradesh, India. The site was declared to be a monument of national importance by the Archaeological Survey of India in June 2019.

items.note: Umaraha is a census town in Varanasi tehsil of Varanasi district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. The census town & village falls under the Umaraha gram panchayat.

Vyaspur is a village in Chiraigaon block of Varanasi district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. It is about 219 kilometers from the state capital Lucknow and 809 kilometers from the national capital Delhi.
Deer Park (Hindi: डियर पार्क) is a mini zoo in Sarnath, Varanasi, Uttar Pradesh, India. Deer Park is also known as Sarnath Deer Park (Hindi: सारनाथ डियर पार्क).

Dhamek Stupa (also spelled Dhamekh and Dhamekha) is a massive stupa located in Deer Park at Sarnath in the state of Uttar Pradesh, India. One of the eight most important pilgrimage sites for Buddhists, the Dhamek Stupa marks the location where the Buddha gave his first teaching to his first five disciples Kaundinya, Assaji, Bhaddiya, Vappa and Mahanama.
Sarnath Jain Tirth, also called the Shreyanshnath Jain Pilgrimage, is a group of Jain temples in Sarnath. They are located near Dhamek Stupa.

Sarnath (also known as Deer Park, Sarangnath, Isipatana Deer Park, Rishipattana, Migadaya, or Mrigadava) is a town northeast of Varanasi, in Uttar Pradesh, India. It was here that the Buddha gave his first sermon after attaining enlightenment at Bodh Gaya.

Sarnath Museum, or Archaeological Museum Sarnath, is the oldest site museum of the Archaeological Survey of India. It houses the findings and excavations at the archaeological site of Sarnath, by the Archaeological Survey of India.

Chaukhandi Stupa is a Buddhist stupa in Sarnath located 8 kilometres from Cantt Railway Station in Varanasi, Uttar Pradesh, India. The site was declared to be a monument of national importance by the Archaeological Survey of India in June 2019.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Swarved Mahamandir?
faq.a: The best time to visit Swarved Mahamandir is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Swarved Mahamandir?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Lal Bahadur Shastri Airport, Varanasi (22 km) and the nearest railway station is Varanasi Junction (12 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Swarved Mahamandir?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Swarved Mahamandir at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Swarved Mahamandir?
faq.a: Stays in Swarved Mahamandir start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Swarved Mahamandir?
faq.a: The top attractions are Umarha, Vyaspur, Sarnath Deer Park, Dhamek Stupa, Sarnath Jain Tirth.
